item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Saturday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
same but i don't usually wear leather pants.. greetings online friends
wow great "party"
moving far away from hometown helps
solid 7.6/10
they forgot to put the milk in the bowls
gourmet so you know it's good
that sounds bad
have never tried a bit-o-honey
that's one of the best i've seen
sorry lady.. Eric is funny
how convenient that it's so close.. going w Creepshow
what else would it be named fo- ... ohhhhhhhhhh
i mean, yeah. but i need to learn magic real quick
mmm hmm
dislike
really is piss poor caretaking
i guess
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: someSundayProgrammingHumorForANicheAudience
item.description:
1
2. Them: You can't code with only 3 keys Me:
3.
4
5. Jeeeez, them laptops....
6. That's amazing
7
8. Shouldn't have done that
9. I HALP
10. Are you a DEV or a QA?
11
12. Oh, shit
13
14. Yo, this guy approached me!
And finally I would like to share something from myself. I've been working very hard on Stanley Parable-like game with a touch of GladOS from Portal called Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)/ The demo made to TOP 10 on Steam's demo trending tab! And it features witty signs like this one here. "What?", I hear you ask. "I can read funny signs for free?" YES, yes you can! Try it now on Steam and please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse Thank you, and if this dump made you smile at least once, I am happy with it! : )

item.dc:creator: kixxarse1986

item.title: My mom said she would give me a cookie if I made it to the Front Page (CHAOTIC MEME DUMP)
item.description:
1. Harder
2. Insightful used car-buying advice that everyone should know.
3. A kitten being lured out from under the hood of a car to be rescued
4. "What? Why are you looking at me like that?"
5 *JUMPS AFTER IT*
6. Rocket racoon
7
8. Interesting eye make up
9. Mama Mia what a flipa
10
11
12
13
14. Check out all the duets
15. Thanks to the two people (Hi, Mom and Dad!) who made it to the end of this dump. When I'm not busy making questionable life choices on the internet, I've been pouring my soul into a little passion project. If you got a kick out of this dump, you're going to love my game: "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)." Think "The Stanley Parable" but with even more chaos and quirky characters such this little nihilist pancake. Can you imagine? A first-person game mixed with humor? Anyway, please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked out the game and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just, whatever you do, don't press the big red button!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: The adventure of learning to give my cat subcutaneous fluids...
item.description:
Miilo is 18 and he has kidney disease. He is also my babiest baby and the love of my life. I got him from the Humane Society about 17 and a half years ago. Poor little guy was found as a stray and I can tell he wasn't weaned properly.
A few weeks ago we learned that we're going to have to start giving him subcutaneous fluids. We Don't have the money to go in to the vet each week so we've been trying to learn how to do it ourselves.
We have had several terrible times trying to get the fluids in him because Miilo is a feisty little guy! So we got this hammock and the only place in the house we could find to hang it is the basement. Which feels so creepy.
My spouse - my other love - was going to school to be a surgery tech years ago so it's his job to stab our poor baby with the massive needle.
Turns out Miilo can get his arms out of the hammock so this last time we used Ace bandages to lock him in. It's utterly ridiculous looking! But, we got more than 100cc in him this last time, which is our most successful attempt yet! The crazy thing is we also sedate this little guy with 1 mL of gabapentin two hours before we do the fluids but he is tough! So the whole thing is ridiculous but we're fine-tuning our approach and Miilo seems soooo much more comfortable when we actually do get the fluids in him. Seeing an 18 year old cat getting the zoomies is pretty great! If anyone has more suggestions on how to get fluids into a feisty cat, let me know!!!! ***Most viral edit: thank you so much for all the suggestions, love, and support! I was trying to figure out how to add a picture of him OUT of the hammock but it doesn't seem like I can add pictures so I'll just post a couple separately. Thank you again!
item.dc:creator: DinoTherapy411
